name: Process expense reimbursements

description: Explaining policies and procedures related to reimbursements requests by employees. Set policies regarding reimbursement process and amount limits etc. Inform employees.
name: Establish and communicate expense reimbursement policies and approval limits

description: Collecting and reporting all pertinent information regarding the taxes paid by the organization's employees.
name: Capture and report relevant tax data

description: Permitting expense reimbursement requests from employees.
name: Approve reimbursements and advances

description: Paying for expense reimbursement requests from employees. (Follow Approve reimbursements and advances [10882] according to policies and conditions.)
name: Process reimbursements and advances

description: Maintaining accounts of individuals who are connected with business.
name: Manage personnel accounts

description: Handling and authoring credit cards to business entities or for corporate purchases.
name: Manage corporate credit cards

description: Developing procedures for using company credit cards. Set or approve credit limits.
name: Establish corporate credit card policies and approval limits

description: Handling applications credit card applications for business expenses.
name: Process corporate credit card requests

description: Obtaining credit cards for business-related expenses.
name: Order corporate credit cards

description: Handling credit card accounts of business customers.
name: Manage corporate credit card accounts

description: Authorizing changes to the available credit advances.
name: Approve/Change credit limits

description: Blocking an existing credit card to disable all future transactions.
name: Cancel/Deactivate credit card

description: Managing business's investments in trading in bonds, currencies, financial derivatives, etc. Manage the financial assets and holdings of the organization. Optimize the organization's liquidity. Invest excess cash. Reduce financial risks.
name: Manage treasury operations

description: Managing rules and regulations for investments in trading in bonds, currencies, financial derivatives, etc. Establish policies and procedures for investments made. Optimize liquidity in treasury operations.
name: Manage treasury policies and procedures

description: Selecting opportunities and the authoritative body for investments in trading in bonds, currencies, financial derivatives, etc.
name: Establish scope and governance of treasury operations

description: Creating and providing investment regulations for the organization. Establish policies and procedures for investments to optimize liquidity in treasury operations. Create a written copy of it.
name: Establish and publish treasury policies

description: Making processes for investing. Create steps for investments in bonds, currencies, and financial derivatives in order to optimize company's liquidity, invest excess cash, and reduce its financial risks.
name: Develop treasury procedures

description: Checking treasury processes in order to optimize company's liquidity, invest excess cash, and reduce its financial risks.
name: Monitor treasury procedures

description: Auditing the treasury function.
name: Audit treasury procedures

description: Reassessing all treasury procedures based on audit findings.
name: Revise treasury procedures

description: Creating and managing the internal control systems for investments in bonds, currencies, and financial derivatives to verify procedures.
name: Develop and confirm internal controls for treasury

description: Describing the need of system security requirements for controlling access, reliability of information, accountability, and availability of information in the organization.
name: Define system security requirements

description: Taking care of all cash-related activities in the business. Manage and reconcile cash positions. Manage cash equivalents. Process and oversee electronic fund transfers. Develop cash flow forecasts. Manage cash flows. Produce cash management accounting transactions and reports. Manage and oversee banking relationships. Analyze, negotiate, resolve, and confirm bank fees.
name: Manage cash

description: Correcting cash differences in the books of accounts. Make optimum utilization of funds available in the business. Check for differences to rectify.
name: Manage and reconcile cash positions

description: Taking care of all cash-related activities in the business. Utilize short-term assets that can be easily convertible into cash, such as marketable securities, commercial paper and short-term government bonds, and treasury bills.
name: Manage cash equivalents

description: Supervising all online transactions.
name: Process and oversee electronic fund transfers (EFTs)

description: Preparing forecasts for the cash generated or used by the organization.
name: Develop cash flow forecasts

description: Delaying the outflow of funds as long as possible, but encourage the inflow of as fast as possible.
name: Manage cash flows

description: Presenting reports on all cash-related activities. Collect and manage short-term investing activities. Prepare reports of all transactions done.
name: Produce cash management accounting transactions and reports

description: Maintaining and directing the course of relationships with banking partners.
name: Manage and oversee banking relationships

description: Studying and finalizing bank fees for services provided by banks. Negotiate and finalize nominal fees that bank charges for various services, such as requesting a deposit slip or counter check or certifying papers.
name: Analyze, negotiate, resolve, and confirm bank fees

description: Managing financial services provided by an in-house bank structure in the corporation that is operating like a commercial bank.
name: Manage in-house bank accounts

description: Maintaining subsidiaries' company accounts opened with bank inside the corporation. Manage different financial services provided by in-house bank structure for parent companies' subsidiaries or branches.
name: Manage in-house bank accounts for subsidiaries

description: Arranging loans for subsidiaries from in-house banks.
name: Manage and facilitate inter-company borrowing transactions

description: Handling payments made for subsidiaries by parent company.
name: Manage centralized outgoing payments on behalf of subsidiaries

description: Handling payments received by parent company for subsidiaries.
name: Manage central incoming payments on behalf of subsidiaries

description: Taking care of all business outflows and recording as whole. Manage making all payments for the organization and its units or subsidiaries. Track in books of accounts of parent company.
name: Manage internal payments and netting transactions

description: Computing all expenses paid to and receivables collected over the organization's banking activity. Calculate all charges and receivables, towards interest, fees, and any other payments over its own bank accounts. Record transactions in the books of accounts.
name: Calculate interest and fees for in-house bank accounts

description: Facilitating account statements for all in-house banking activity.
name: Provide account statements for in-house bank accounts

description: Taking care of the organization's financial position. Manage its loans or debts from different sources and investments. Leverage the most profitable options to balance the financial position in the market.
name: Manage debt and investment

description: Developing and instituting principles that the organization ill use in making investments.
name: Establish investment policy

description: Maintaining smooth relations with financial investment banks that help availing loans and services.
name: Manage financial intermediary relationships

description: Managing and maintaining enough liquidity in form of cash and cash equivalents in the business to meet urgent and timely requirements
name: Manage liquidity

description: Managing the exposure incurred by the issuer for providing credit to the borrower.
name: Manage issuer exposure

description: Tracking loans taken and money invested in different options. Arrange and supervise loans from banks and individuals and investments in different available and profitable options.
name: Process and oversee debt and investment transactions

description: Arranging and supervising foreign exchange rate changes to avoid loss on foreign-currency transactions.
name: Process and oversee foreign currency transactions

description: Creating transactions report of loans and investments. Prepare and maintain records of loans and investment transactions.
name: Produce debt and investment accounting transaction reports

description: Supervising the interest paid or received by the organization. Arrange and supervise interest rate swap transactions to manage exposure to fluctuations in interest rates. Or attain a marginally lower rate of interest than could be gained through a swap.
name: Process and oversee interest rate transactions

description: Performing transactions that limit investment risk with the help of derivatives, such as options and futures contracts. Manage interest rates, foreign exchange, and exposure risks. Develop and execute hedging transactions. Evaluate and refine hedging positions. Produce hedge accounting transactions and reports. Monitor credit.
name: Monitor and execute risk and hedging transactions

description: Taking an investment position to offset exposure to certain risks. This may include purchasing opposite of the organization's position in the marketplace, using derivatives transactions, or futures contracts.
name: Develop risk management/hedging strategy

description: Handling risks arising from changes in the interest rate.
name: Manage interest rate risk

description: Collecting and storing data that pertains to interest rate markets.
name: Manage interest rate market data

description: Identifying potential interest rate risks for all markets.
name: Determine interest rate exposure for all markets

description: Deciding the requirements on interest rate investments that are made by trading in futures or options market, on the basis of the accepted risk policy.
name: Determine interest rate hedge requirements in accordance with risk policy

description: Performing trading on interest rates.
name: Execute interest rate trades

description: Taking care of foreign-exchange risks.
name: Manage foreign exchange risk

description: Handling and processing information about changes in foreign exchange rates.
name: Manage foreign exchange market data

description: Establishing potential foreign exchange risks for all currencies.
name: Determine foreign exchange exposure for all currencies

description: Deciding the requirements on investments in foreign exchange made by trading in futures or options market, on the basis of current risk policy.
name: Determine foreign exchange hedge requirements in accordance with risk policy

description: Executing all aspects for foreign exchange trade within foreign exchange market. This includes buying, selling, and exchanging currencies at the current or expressed price point.
name: Execute foreign exchange trades

description: Overseeing the foreign exchange balance sheet with an eye towards potential risk. Risks include changes in conversion rates between the time the transaction occurred and when it is completed, or when transactions are made in a denomination other than that of the organization's base currency.
name: Manage foreign exchange balance sheet risk

description: Taking care of exposure risks. Maintain financial investments in particular investments or a portfolios that could be risky for the organization.
name: Manage exposure risk

description: Establishing ongoing risks that the customers face, and the exceptions to exceeded limits.
name: Determine current customer exposures and limit exceptions

description: Settling cases that involve violations of customer exposure limit.
name: Resolve customer exposure limit violations

description: Handling customer securities to recover loans that are not paid back.
name: Manage customer collateral

description: Conducting reviews to assess customer credit on a yearly basis. Introduce changes or make recommendations, if needed.
name: Perform annual customer credit reviews

description: Implementing hedging strategy in attempt to alleviate risk. This will include all options, depravities, and futures contracts agreed upon in Develop risk management/hedging strategy [12974].
name: Execute hedging transactions

description: Evaluating investments made in some market to offset the risks of investing in a contrary or opposing market.
name: Measure physical positions

description: Determining which hedge options to execute.
name: Establish hedges

description: Closing out a position or cashing in derivatives early.
name: Unwind hedges

description: Examining options in the market for hedging investments. Select an option.
name: Evaluate and refine hedging positions

description: Revising credit reports periodically for accurateness and changes that could be suggestive of duplicitous activity.
name: Monitor credit

description: Preparing and documenting accounts and records of all hedging investment transactions to reduce risks due to change in markets.
name: Produce hedge accounting transactions and reports

description: Handling cases that involve financial fraud. Resolve disputes.
name: Manage financial fraud/dispute cases

description: Administering internal controls. This process requires the organization to manage entity's board of trustees, management, and other personnel in order to offer judicious assurance about the achievement of effectiveness, proficiency of operations, and reliability of financial reporting.
name: Manage internal controls

description: Forming rules and regulations to ensure the achievement of effectiveness, proficiency of operations, and reliability of financial reporting.
name: Establish internal controls, policies, and procedures

description: Establishing board of directors and auditing committee in order to assign roles and responsibilities for internal controls.
name: Establish board of directors and audit committee

description: Outlining and communicating a code of ethics act responsibly.
name: Define and communicate code of ethics

description: Defining roles, responsibilities, and accountabilities for effectiveness and proficiency of operations and reliability of financial reporting.
name: Assign roles and responsibility for internal controls

description: Outlining the objectives and risks associated with a process. Delineate process goals. Determine the risks attached to it. Determine what the process is meant to accomplish, potential issues, a timeline of potential risks, the scope and potential impact of risks, etc.
name: Define business process objectives and risks

description: Outlining the risk tolerance levels of individual units, as well as the organization as a whole. Determine the specific maximum risk to take in quantitative terms for each relevant risk subcategory, including strategic, operational, financial, and compliance risks.
name: Define entity/unit risk tolerances

description: Performing planning, management, operations, and monitoring of internal control mechanism policies and procedures in order to manage internal controls.
name: Operate controls and monitor compliance with internal controls policies and procedures

description: Overseeing the activities for internal controls. Observe the effectiveness of policies, procedures, techniques, and mechanisms actions taken to minimize risk.
name: Monitor control effectiveness

description: Taking corrective measures for policies, procedures, techniques, and mechanisms actions taken to minimize risk. (Conduct in accordance with Monitor control effectiveness [10918] in order to determine and rectify the control deficiencies.)
name: Remediate control deficiencies

description: Reporting on internal controls compliance to the appropriate authority, including IT regulations and pertinent data.
name: Report on internal controls compliance

description: Reporting to external auditors. This process requires the organization to report to external auditors about the regulations for any critical data that the organization is holding.
name: Report to external auditors

description: Reporting to regulators, shareholders, debt holders, securities exchanges, etc. about IT regulations and pertinent data.
name: Report to regulators, share-/debt-holders, securities exchanges, etc.

description: Reporting to suppliers, customers, and partners that are doing business with the company about IT regulations and pertinent data.
name: Report to third parties

description: Reporting to internal management (all employees, directors, and management) about IT regulations and pertinent data.
name: Report to internal management

description: Estimating the organization's periodic tax liabilities. Ensure that appropriate taxing authorities receive tax return filings and payments when due.
name: Manage taxes

description: Setting targets for periodic tax liabilities. Assess the tax impact of various activities such as the acquisition or disposal of fixed assets or a deliberate change in number of employee.
name: Develop tax strategy and plan

description: Developing a tax strategy for foreign, national, state, local administration. Set up tax strategies for foreign trade in imports and exports and at national, state, and local level.
name: Develop foreign, national, state, and local tax strategy

description: Combining and enhancing a rational analysis of a financial condition or plan from a tax perspective in order to align financial goals through efficient tax planning.
name: Consolidate and optimize total tax plan

description: Maintaining a master file about the rational analysis of a financial condition or plan from a tax perspective in order to align financial goals through efficient tax planning.
name: Maintain tax master data

description: Processing the taxes of the organization in line with the regional taxation structure, including corporate, property, excise, and service taxes.
name: Process taxes

description: Creating and implementing strategies for taxes to be paid or collected by the business.
name: Perform tax planning/strategy

description: Preparing and submitting tax reports for every employee to the tax department in order to show the tax paid and deducted from their salaries in the year.
name: Prepare tax returns

description: Preparing reports about paid or accrued foreign taxes to an overseas country.
name: Prepare foreign taxes

description: Calculating the income that has been realized when the tax on that income has not.
name: Calculate deferred taxes

description: Managing the organization's financial accounts for the purpose of taxation. Prepare and maintain the tax paid by the organization to the country they have business in.
name: Account for taxes

description: Checking and correcting the tax policies according to the rules and regulations set by the organization.
name: Monitor tax compliance

description: Addressing any tax queries by any regulatory or government authorities. Review historical records related to taxation within the organization in order to respond to queries.
name: Address tax inquiries

description: Managing cash collections and disbursements made by operating units across the enterprise. When appropriate, transfer cash from the operating units to parent-level bank accounts managed by the organization's treasury team.
name: Manage international funds/consolidation

description: Forecasting and monitoring changes in foreign currency value or interest rates around the world that play an important role in the organization.
name: Monitor international rates

description: Managing any transfer of funds in the course of conducting cross-border trades or investments, including conversion across currencies. Find the most suitable alternative for making payments, while saving taxes and avoiding any unwarranted regulation, with the objective of protecting capital.
name: Manage transactions

description: Assessing exposure to potential financial losses as a result of changes in the value of currencies. Forecast the impact of movements in foreign currency values. Enter into financial transactions designed to offset or limit potential exposure to loss.
name: Monitor currency exposure/hedge currency

description: Documenting and reporting accounting entries to formally report financial gains or losses experienced as a result of foreign exchange activity.
name: Report results

description: Making and collecting payments for transactions in products/services, and transporting them to interested markets.
name: Perform global trade services

description: Evaluating the approved list of parties for engaging in international trade in order to ensure the safety of the organization's business transactions. Examine agents that have been granted legal rights to engage in global trade and their credentials.
name: Screen sanctioned party list

description: Overseeing and directing the flow of trade to/from the organization in order to ensure financial gains.
name: Control exports and imports

description: Systematically categorizing products/services for their suitability to international trade. Create classes and categories for demarcating the types of products suitable for international trade. Study requisite national and international standards and the adherence of the organization's portfolio of offerings to these.
name: Classify products

description: Identifying current exchange rates between two currencies and converting the foreign currency to that of the local monetary unit. I.E., yen to US dollar.
name: Perform currency conversion

description: Computing the excise duty to be paid during international trade.
name: Calculate duty

description: Communicating with the customs department to ensure fluid compliance. Share pertinent information mandated by law with the government agency that controls and collects the duties levied for the international exchange of products/services.
name: Communicate with customs

description: Documenting and recording the trade processes while making transactions, noting the description, quality, number, transportation medium, indemnity, and inspection.
name: Document trade

description: Preparing global trade under preference, which allows the organization to import/export products at a lower or nil rate of customs duty and/or levy charge.
name: Process trade preferences

description: Administering and overseeing all restitution activities the organization may be subjected to. Manage compliance with apposite legal frameworks. Make any restitution that may be required by law; comply with authorities over any fines or non-financial measures imposed.
name: Handle restitution

description: Creating a document assuring that a seller will receive payment when certain delivery conditions are met. (If the buyer is unable to make payment on the purchase, a bank covers the outstanding amount.)
name: Prepare letter of credit

description: Relating to the design, construction, acquisition, and management of both productive and non-productive assets.
name: Acquire, Construct, and Manage Assets

description: Planning, acquiring, and managing facilities, workspaces, and supporting assets. Acquire, configure, and manage facilities and workspaces, to include supporting equipment and materials.
name: Plan and acquire assets

description: Strategizing a long-term vision for managing properties. Prepare strategies and a long-term vision for managing purchased/retained properties.
name: Develop property strategy and long term vision

description: Creating alignment between the requirement of properties and the overall business strategy. This process requires the organization to align the requirement of properties in accordance with its business strategies of the organization.
name: Confirm alignment of property requirements with business strategy

description: Evaluating the impact of the external environment. Evaluate the circumstances, objects, events, and aspects surrounding an organization that affects its actions and selections and that recognizes its opportunities and risks.
name: Appraise the external environment

description: Deciding whether to buy or build properties. Study the market forces about property prices and cost of construction in order to take decisions based on the market research.
name: Determine build, buy, or lease decision

description: Recognizing the needs of facility users in order to construct a project proposal that meets those needs.
name: Plan facility

description: Preparing and analyzing different designs for a facility in order to finalize which design will be the most suitable option.
name: Design facility

description: Evaluating the feasibility of budgets prepared for the construction of facilities.
name: Analyze budget

description: Assessing and choosing the appropriate property. Analyze the property requirements. Review the available property options. Finalize the most suitable option.
name: Select property

description: Discussing the terms and conditions of facilities to be occupied according to the business requirements and availability of budgets.
name: Negotiate terms for facility

description: Constructing the buildings. Manage renovations according to requirements and demands.
name: Manage construction or modification to building

description: Managing the provision of the workspace and its assets. Arrange an office space with all assets (tables, chairs, computers, admin staff, etc.) according to requirements.
name: Provide workspace and facilities

description: Attaining the office space with all assets (tables, chairs, computers, admin staff, etc.) according to requirements.
name: Acquire workspace and facilities

description: Modifying the formation of the workspace and its assets. Make necessary changes in an office space with all assets (tables, chairs, computers, admin staff, interior designing, etc.) according to requirements.
name: Change fit/form/function of workspace and facilities

description: Managing all operational activities of the facility. Manage how each function/business unit works. Support the manufacturing facility to attain organizational goals.
name: Manage facilities operations

description: Shifting staff or employees from one place to another place according to changes in business requirements.
name: Relocate people

description: Relocating the tools and raw materials. Shift raw or finished material and machines of company from one place to another place according to changes in business requirements.
name: Relocate material and tools

description: AI-era activity: AI-Powered Predictive Maintenance.
name: AI-Powered Predictive Maintenance

description: Designing and constructing assets such as machines, tools, factories, etc. Manage steps to acquire assets including managing capital, as well as planning, scheduling, and overseeing acquisition/construction.
name: Design and construct assets

description: Producing and maintaining a planning schedule and a financial plan to purchase or manufacture productive assets. Determine the investment plan, monitor capital, and secure the necessary financing in order to realize completion of the program.
name: Manage capital program for assets

description: Establishing what funds will be invested in the construction of productive assets for the advancement of established business objectives.
name: Define capital investment plan

description: Monitoring plans on capital projects. Capital projects can be purchasing buildings, lands, etc.
name: Monitor capital program

description: Acquiring the loans needed to construct necessary assets.
name: Secure construction financing

description: Outlining the steps and strategies needed to construct assets. Verify that all regulations are adhered to and that all permissions have been granted. Organize and plan for resources to complete construction.
name: Design and plan asset construction

description: Developing a strategy to perform asset construction. Assure that timelines, regulations, and resources are on task and on budget.
name: Develop construction strategy

description: Managing the construction process to ensure that activates are on task, on budget, and are being performed with safety and quality in mind.
name: Perform construction performance management

description: Gathering the required permits for construction from the proper jurisdiction. This may include inspections during and after construction to verify that the new asset meets all national, regional, and local codes.
name: Obtain construction permissions

description: Designing assets to meet organizational needs as well as ensuring that the asset adheres to all national, regional, and local construction codes.
name: Design assets

description: Determining what resources will need to be acquired in order to carry out construction. Plan when, where, and how resources will be used. Determine the length of time resources will be utilized.
name: Plan construction resources

description: Arranging a timetable for which to perform construction work. Schedule resources to contract assets for new or replacement assets. Reschedule or redesign assets if needed.
name: Schedule and perform construction work

description: Defining a timetable for which to execute the construction of the asset.
name: Schedule construction work

description: Gathering resources needed to complete all construction work. Verify that all resources have the proper training and skills to perform the work.
name: Obtain resources

description: Building new assets necessary for the organization. Be aware of any construction codes and permits that need to be addressed.
name: Construct new assets

description: Modifying existing assets to align with the changing needs of the organization. Be aware of any construction codes and permits that need to be addressed.
name: Augment existing assets

description: Determining the need to replace existing assets. Be aware of any construction codes and permits that need to be addressed.
name: Renew/Replace assets

description: Overseeing the performance and quality of work. Assure that records are maintained throughout the construction process. Adhere to all safety, security, and access regulations set forth by the organization and all government standards.
name: Manage asset construction

description: Monitoring construction to insure that all regulatory laws are being adhered to, that all work is being performed in a timely manner, and that quality assurance is met at all steps of the construction process.
name: Monitor work performance

description: Implementing a checks and balances system to verify that the construction was performed correctly. Rework when errors are found.
name: Undertake construction quality control

description: Implementing records to include all construction work that has been performed. Include all new or modified construction, and any construction or regulatory issues that might have occurred.
name: Create work and asset records

description: Ensuring that safety, security, and access is maintained. Provide a workplace that meets and exceeds all local, state, and federal guidelines. Provide security and access to the building site as set forth by safety and organizational guidelines.
name: Manage safety, security, and access to sites

description: Preserving productive assets through the planning, managing, and performance of preventative, routine, and critical maintenance work.
name: Maintain assets

description: Ensuring that necessary resources are available and tasks are prioritized accordingly through planning. Provide strategies and policies that identify tasks that need to be completed, and the resources necessary to fulfill those tasks.
name: Plan asset maintenance

description: Creating goals and agendas to better realize the success of the maintenance policies that have been put into place.
name: Develop maintenance strategies

description: Evaluating assets in order to project future requirements for maintenance. Evaluate the present working condition of assets. Determine the future maintenance requirements of assets.
name: Analyze assets and predict maintenance requirements

description: Communicating policies in regards to asset maintenance. Provide a clear set of procedures and policies that outline what will be involved in the maintenance process.
name: Specify maintenance policies

description: Devising a methodology and procedure for assimilating the works of planned maintenance into the schedule scheme for the processing of finished products that utilizes the same machinery.
name: Integrate preventive maintenance into operations schedule

description: Identifying the steps needed for asset maintenance. List out the those tasks that are involved in completing this process. Prioritize the tasks that are created.
name: Identify work management tasks & priorities

description: Analyzing workload needs in relation to asset maintenance and plan resources around those needs.
name: Conduct resource planning

description: Creating procedures on how to maintain productive assets.
name: Create work plans

description: Ensuring that asset maintenance is conducted in a timely manner and successfully. Schedule work with the required resources with an eye on quality control and safety. Verify that contracted maintenance meets performance targets.
name: Manage asset maintenance

description: Defining a timetable for which to execute the maintenance of the asset.
name: Schedule maintenance work

description: Gathering resources needed to complete all maintenance work. Verify that all resources have the proper training and skills to perform the work.
name: Obtain required resources

description: Implementing a checks and balances system to verify that the maintenance was performed correctly. Rework when errors are found.
name: Undertake quality control

description: Modifying existing maintenance records to include all new work that has been performed, what assets were serviced, and any issues that might have arisen.
name: Update work and asset records

description: Assuring that all safety laws and regulations are being implemented and followed. Align practices with regulatory bureaus such as OSHA , EHS, and ISO.
name: Manage maintenance work safety

description: Outlining what should be achieved through predictive indicators with regard to performing maintenance. This could include the length of time it takes to perform routine maintenance or how often unplanned maintenance feasibly occur.
name: Define maintenance performance targets

description: Following set performance targets, monitor and gage the success of the organization in meeting those targets.
name: Monitor maintenance performance against targets/contracts

description: Engaging in all aspects of asset maintenance. Ensure that all assets are functioning properly and to all specified codes and regulations where applicable. Maintenance includes all preventative, routine, and corrective activates.
name: Perform asset maintenance

description: Performing prophylactic maintenance in an effort to avoid corrective or unplanned repairs.
name: Perform preventative asset maintenance

description: Carrying out required maintenance to continue upkeep of equipment or assets.
name: Perform routine asset maintenance

description: Repairing or correcting faults that occur with an asset. This could be a break or other repairable damage.
name: Perform corrective asset maintenance and repairs

description: Realizing potential or current problems with assets that would require unplanned maintenance. Unplanned maintenance is a repair or change that needs to be made that is not preventative or routine.
name: Identify unplanned maintenance requirements

description: Performing repairs that occur outside of normal routine or preventative maintenance.
name: Perform unplanned maintenance and repairs

description: Managing the disposition of assets at their end-of-life. End-of-life for an asset may be due to obsolescence, replacement, excess capacity, or disrepair among other potential factors. Steps may include decommissioning, disassembly, recycling, etc. Tracking and reporting may also be appropriate for some or all asset types due to law, regulation, or social/environmental responsibility.
name: Manage asset end-of-life

description: Creating a strategy for managing asset exits.
name: Develop exit strategy

description: Monitoring assets against their planned useful life. Each asset may have specific criteria that indicate their usability. This may include time-based, maintenance history, capacity planning, etc.
name: Monitor useful life of assets

description: Cost/benefit analysis of assets to determine retention or end-of-life. Evaluating asset total lifecycle cost against other factors such as repair/refurbishment, replacement, and alternate solutions.
name: Perform cost vs. benefit analysis for replacement

description: Retiring assets that are no longer viable to the business. Decommission assets that are no longer in working order, are out of date, or whose maintenance exceeds the cost of replacement.
name: Decommission productive assets

description: Performing the sale of assets. Achieve and complete the sale process. Deliver the end product to the customers.
name: Perform sale or trade

description: Managing locations where end-of-life assets are evaluated for decommissioning, disassembly, recycling, etc.
name: Manage take-back centers

description: Dismantling an asset into manageable parts. The parts may follow different dispositions, such as resale, reuse, recycle, etc.
name: Dismantle assets

description: Tracking the disposition of all parts from end-of-life assets. Tracking should provide an audit trail to include source, rational for disposition, and destination.
name: Track parts

description: Recycling parts in accordance with legal, regulatory, and social/environmental responsibilities.
name: Recycle parts

description: Planning, conducting, and tracking shipment of hazardous materials.
name: Ship hazardous material

description: Preparing and distributing government reports.
name: Provide government reporting

description: Planning and performing abandonment activities. Whether facilities  or equipment, ensure assets to be abandoned are safe, secure, and meet all legal, regulatory, and social/environmental responsibilities.
name: Perform abandonment

description: Demolishing and securing asset and resulting parts/debris.
name: Demolish and secure

description: Performing remediation or restoration activities.
name: Restore site

description: Managing and reporting site status. Align efforts to all legal, regulatory and social/environmental responsibilities.
name: Monitor site for duration of time required by regulators

description: Supporting handover to new operator or landowner. Include proper documentation on asset history and current state to enable orderly transition.
name: Prepare handover to new operator or landowner

description: Planning and performing waste and hazardous goods management. In compliance with all legal, regulatory, and social/environmental requirements, plan, document, and conduct hazardous material handling, disposal, and reporting.
name: Perform waste and hazardous goods management

description: Preparing material safety sheets. Capture material safety records to properly apply handling and disposal requirements.
name: Maintain material safety data sheets

description: Understanding and communicating hazardous material regulatory requirements.
name: Maintain awareness of and communicate regulatory requirements

description: Planning, overseeing, and tracking hazardous material handling and disposal.
name: Maintain hazardous material handling and disposal

description: Planning, supporting, and documenting hazardous material audits.
name: Manage compliance audits

description: Identifying and establishing approaches to dispose of hazardous materials/waste.
name: Recognize need to disposition hazardous materials/waste

description: Evaluating hazardous materials and waste for appropriate disposition. Apply requirements and initiate disposition activities.
name: Determine disposition and associated processing

description: Performing disposition, disposal, and reprocessing activities.
name: Manage disposition, disposal, reprocessing activities

description: Documenting and reporting disposition, disposal, and reprocessing activities.
name: Manage documentation and reporting

description: Ensuring that an organization effectively manages its risk. Process groups are aligned with traditional risk management activities.
name: Manage Enterprise Risk, Compliance, Remediation, and Resiliency

description: Creating requisite frameworks and coordinating all risk management activities for the entire organization and each function. Manage the enterprise risk by outlining the risk policies and procedures. Monitor and communicate all risk management activities. Encourage correspondence among the business units. Manage the risk of all business units and functions.
name: Manage enterprise risk

description: Creating an agenda for the rules and regulations of enterprise risk that deal with hazardous, financial, operational, and strategic risks.
name: Establish the enterprise risk framework and policies

description: Recognizing the organization's tolerance for risk, given risk-return trade-offs for one or more anticipated and predictable consequences.
name: Determine risk tolerance for organization

description: Establishing and maintaining the policies and procedures for managing risk. Create rules and regulations for enterprise risk dealing with hazardous, financial, operational, and strategic risks.
name: Develop and maintain enterprise risk policies and procedures

description: Recognizing and implementing tools for managing risk. Identify and apply enterprise risk management tools. Leverage methods and processes to manage risks and opportunities associated with business objectives.
name: Identify and implement enterprise risk management tools

description: Communicating the knowledge about risk within the organization. Identify operational risks. Share risk information within the organization.
name: Coordinate the sharing of risk knowledge across the organization

description: Preparing and presenting reports about enterprise risk to the management of the organization. Create reports for management on hazard risks (e.g., property damage and liability torts), financial risks (e.g., currency and liquidity risks), and operational risks (e.g., product failure, customer satisfaction, social trends, and competition).
name: Prepare and report enterprise risk to executive management and board

description: Coordinating to plan, organize, lead, and control the activities of an organization in order to minimize the special effects of risk on capital and earnings.
name: Oversee and coordinate enterprise risk management activities

description: Determining risks that could thwart objectives. Document and communicate the concern.
name: Identify enterprise level risks

description: Identifying options/actions to enhance opportunities and reduce threats. Recognize the root reasons of the identified risks.
name: Assess risks to determine which to mitigate

description: Developing activities to improve opportunities and lessen threats. Specify the organization's objectives. Evolve strategies and policies to attain these objectives. Assign resources to project objectives.
name: Develop risk mitigation and management strategy and integrate with existing performance management processes

description: Checking that the blueprint created for managing risk in individual business units and divisions is correctly effectuated. Validate the implementation of all activities geared to mitigate risks.
name: Verify business unit and functional risk mitigation plans are implemented

description: Ensuring risk monitoring and mitigation activities. Monitor actions to enhance opportunities and reduce threats to project objectives.
name: Ensure risks and risk mitigation actions are monitored

description: Creating a report of activities to address hazard risks, liability torts, financial risks, operational risks, social trends, competition, etc.
name: Report on enterprise risk activities

description: Coordinating risk management activities to improve opportunities and lessen threats. Specify the organization's objectives. Assign resources to project objectives.
name: Coordinate business unit and functional risk management activities

description: Checking each business unit's/function's options and activities to improve opportunities and lessen threats.
name: Ensure that each business unit/function follows the enterprise risk management process

description: Checking the reporting process of each business unit's/function's options and activities to improve opportunities and lessen threats.
name: Ensure that each business unit/function follows the enterprise risk reporting process

description: Analyzing the threats a business unit/function faces to prioritize the controls it implements..
name: Manage business unit and function risk

description: Developing a timely and continuous process to identify activities that might hinder a project's goals.
name: Identify risks

description: Determining the possibility that a specified undesirable event will occur using established tools, implements, and frameworks. Use risk assessments to determine, for example, whether to undertake a particular venture, what rate of return a particular investment requires, and how to mitigate an activity's potential losses.
name: Assess risks using enterprise risk framework policies and procedures

description: Developing possibilities and arrangements to improve opportunities and reduce deviations to project objectives.
name: Develop mitigation plans for risks

description: Evaluating the changing needs for insurance coverage. Research available insurance providers and offerings.
name: Assess adequacy of insurance coverage

description: Executing mitigation plans to improve opportunities and reduce deviations to project objectives.
name: Implement mitigation plans for risks

description: Identifying, examining, and recognizing/justifying any improbability in investment decision making.
name: Monitor risks

description: Examining the impact of risk activities in order to update the existing scheme of risk management. Analyze and substantiate the potential for adverse consequences to occur. Consider the risks associated with the activity and the methods available to manage those risks.
name: Analyze risk activities and update plans

description: Creating reports on risk activities, and communicating them to management. Prepare reports on the potential for adverse safety consequences.
name: Report on risk activities

description: ITIL 4 practice: Risk Management. Mapped to Manage enterprise risk.
name: Risk Management

description: Activity within ITIL practice 'Risk Management'.
name: Assess IT risks

description: Activity within ITIL practice 'Risk Management'.
name: Treat IT risks

description: Activity within ITIL practice 'Risk Management'.
name: Monitor IT risk landscape

description: Managing steps to confirm enduring compliance to industry regulations and government legislation.
name: Manage compliance

description: Developing a set of procedures detailing an organization's progress in complying with established guidelines, provisions, and legislation.
name: Establish compliance framework and policies

description: Creating a standardized approach to ethics and compliance. Have a programmatic approach for compliance that focuses on the definite risks the organization faces.
name: Develop enterprise compliance policies and procedures

description: Implementing standardized for ethics and compliance. Have a programmatic approach, built from the top down, to enterprise compliance that focuses on the definite risks the organization faces.
name: Implement enterprise compliance activities

description: Managing accounts and prepare regular reports on financial performance.
name: Manage internal audits

description: Managing technologies and tools related to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data in order to ensure the security of the organization's information.
name: Maintain controls-related technologies and tools

description: Obeying laws, guidelines, strategies, and stipulations related to the business.
name: Manage regulatory compliance

description: Developing procedures and methodologies to comply with relevant laws and regulations of an organization's obedience to laws, guidelines, strategies and stipulations related to business.
name: Develop regulatory compliance procedures

description: Determining the regulatory requirements that are most appropriate for the organization. Identify goals in order to follow the appropriate rules and regulations, guidelines, and strategies.
name: Identify applicable regulatory requirements

description: Analyzing and overseeing the regulatory environment in order to spot any changing or emerging regulations. This process element calls upon the organization to monitor the regulatory environment for any new statutes, policies, and enactments issued by the respective government authorities or those which have been updated.
name: Monitor the regulatory environment for changing or emerging regulations

description: Evaluating current regulatory policies and regulations. Assess their performance. Make necessary changes.
name: Assess current compliance position and identify weaknesses or shortfalls therein

description: Assessing the current policies and policies. Implement missing and necessary changes environmental changes, political changes, technological changes, etc.
name: Implement missing or stronger regulatory compliance controls and policies

description: Monitoring, appraising, and evaluating the compliance position of the organization in order to fine-tune for effective remediation. Track efforts for handling regulatory and compliance requirements necessitated by law. Test the robustness of internal frameworks, procedures, and approaches for dealing with these requirements, in order to clearly identify any necessary changes.
name: Monitor and test regulatory compliance position and existing controls

description: Creating a graphical representation of metrics in order to communicate the general health of the organization in relation to risk and compliancy.
name: Compile and communicate compliance scorecard(s)

description: Submitting compliance reports to regulatory agencies. These reports can be made to environmental, securities, or human resources agencies as stipulated by the local governing body.
name: Compile and communicate internal and regulatory compliance reports

description: Developing and preserving relationships with the regulators, without compromising the legal basis of the relationship.
name: Maintain relationships with regulators as appropriate

description: AI-era activity: AI-Enhanced Compliance Monitoring.
name: AI-Enhanced Compliance Monitoring

description: Administering the efforts and activities for remediation. This process element requires the organization to create plans for corrective action in collaboration with government agencies and pertinent professional services agencies which specialize in remediation efforts relevant to the organization's operations. Additionally, the organization needs to consult experts to validate the plan, determine resources allocation, resolve any legal concerns, and formulate a company-wide policy for remediation.
name: Manage remediation efforts

description: Creating plans for remediation efforts. Make a plan to address a case of environmental adulteration. Identify and treat the adulteration so that the area will become operational again.
name: Create remediation plans

description: Discussing and soliciting advice from experts for in order to incorporate their suggestion (regarding Create remediation plans [11201]).
name: Contact and confer with experts

description: Identifying and dedicating the resources for managing remediation efforts. Discern the resources needed for remediation efforts. Dispense with resources in a sound and well-reasoned manner.
name: Identify/dedicate resources

description: Examining regulatory and legislative frameworks. Obligate the organization to remediate any damages through compensations, fines, and any other remedial efforts necessitated to correct the situations. Analyze local environmental laws, binding international covenants, etc. in order to examine legal accuracy about the rules and procedures.
name: Investigate legal aspects

description: Studying the causes of damage, which could be environmental, physical, social, etc. at country level in order to institute better policies and regulations.
name: Investigate damage cause

description: Crafting a new framework of policies and procedures for deploying remediation efforts, or change existing policies and procedures. Adapt the policy structure to the context of the apposite national and international regulatory frameworks.
name: Amend or create policy

description: Including the processes required to rapidly adapt and respond to any internal or external opportunity, demand, disruption, or threat. Develop a more dynamic, strategic, and integrated approach to managing compliance obligations.
name: Manage business resiliency

description: Creating a strategy for rapidly adapting to disturbances. Maintain continuous business processes and protecting employees, assets, and overall brand equity.
name: Develop the business resilience strategy

description: Developing plans to ensure continuous business operations.
name: Perform continuous business operations planning

description: Assessing ongoing activities within the organization that are not intended to stop except for in an emergency.
name: Test continuous business operations

description: Evaluating business operations. Determine which activities generate revenues, perform best, and provide good returns.
name: Maintain continuous business operations

description: Sharing information about risks and resilience strategies of business operations across the organization so that prospective risks can be avoided.
name: Share knowledge of specific risks across other parts of the organization

description: AI-era process group: AI Governance and Ethics.
name: AI Governance and Ethics

description: AI process: Manage AI Ethics Framework.
name: Manage AI Ethics Framework

description: AI activity: Define AI ethics principles.
name: Define AI ethics principles

description: AI activity: Establish ethics review board.
name: Establish ethics review board

description: AI activity: Conduct AI impact assessments.
name: Conduct AI impact assessments

description: AI activity: Monitor ethical compliance.
name: Monitor ethical compliance

description: AI process: Manage AI Regulatory Compliance.
name: Manage AI Regulatory Compliance

description: AI activity: Monitor AI regulations (EU AI Act, etc.).
name: Monitor AI regulations (EU AI Act, etc.)

description: AI activity: Classify AI systems by risk level.
name: Classify AI systems by risk level

description: AI activity: Maintain AI compliance documentation.
name: Maintain AI compliance documentation

description: AI activity: Conduct regulatory audits.
name: Conduct regulatory audits

description: AI process: Manage AI Safety and Alignment.
name: Manage AI Safety and Alignment

description: AI activity: Define AI safety requirements.
name: Define AI safety requirements

description: AI activity: Implement guardrails and filters.
name: Implement guardrails and filters

description: AI activity: Test adversarial robustness.
name: Test adversarial robustness

description: AI activity: Monitor alignment metrics.
name: Monitor alignment metrics

description: AI process: Manage AI Bias and Fairness.
name: Manage AI Bias and Fairness

description: AI activity: Assess training data bias.
name: Assess training data bias

description: AI activity: Implement fairness metrics.
name: Implement fairness metrics

description: AI activity: Audit model decisions for bias.
name: Audit model decisions for bias

description: AI activity: Remediate identified biases.
name: Remediate identified biases

description: AI process: Manage AI Transparency and Explainability.
name: Manage AI Transparency and Explainability

description: AI activity: Implement model explainability.
name: Implement model explainability

description: AI activity: Document AI decision processes.
name: Document AI decision processes

description: AI activity: Provide stakeholder AI literacy.
name: Provide stakeholder AI literacy

description: AI activity: Publish AI transparency reports.
name: Publish AI transparency reports

description: Fostering external relationships with stakeholders of the entity, including investors, government and industry, the board of directors, and the general public. This is not related to customer management.
name: Manage External Relationships

description: Creating a strategic management responsibility for integrating finance, communication, marketing, and securities law compliance. Allow the most effective two-way communication among the organization, the financial community, and other constituencies. Enlist the investor relations function to provide market intelligence to corporate management.
name: Build investor relationships

description: Building and managing relations with bankers or lenders through strong products/services strategies that bankers would want to invest in. Foster a receptive environment for low rates of interest, easy access to loans, etc.
name: Plan, build, and manage lender relations

description: Creating and maintaining long-term relations with analysts. Involve analysts in strategy and product decisions in order to elicit valuable information.
name: Plan, build, and manage analyst relations

description: Practicing regular, transparent communication with shareholders through annual shareholders' meetings, quarterly earnings calls, shareholders letters, one-on-one emails or calls, etc.
name: Communicate with shareholders

description: AI-era activity: AI-Powered Partner Assessment.
name: AI-Powered Partner Assessment

description: Creating and maintaining relationships with government and industry representatives.
name: Manage government and industry relationships

description: Persuading public and government policy at the local, regional, national, and global level (subject to government regulations).
name: Manage government relations

description: Ascertaining how the business entity relates to all levels of government. Identify areas that needs further growth and resources to foster those relationships.
name: Assess relationships

description: Assigning executive level resources to manage, grow, and drive relationships with government bodies.
name: Appoint responsible executives

description: Analyzing current relationships with government bodies and entities.
name: Monitor relationships

description: Garnering internal advice from an informal group in order to successfully maintain and advance relationships.
name: Receive input from internal advisors

description: Garnering third party advice from an informal group in order to successfully maintain and advance relationships.
name: Receive input from external advisors

description: Meeting with government heads and representatives.
name: Liaise with authorities

description: Managing relations with quasi-governmental organizations, corporations, businesses, or any other agency that is treated by national laws and principles to be under the supervision of the government but also distinct and self-directed from the government.
name: Manage relations with quasi-government bodies

description: Engaging government, regulatory, or industry agencies to establish relationships. Analyze steps and requirements necessary for inclusion, if needed.
name: Establish relationships with agencies

description: Cooperating with auditors regarding potential or ongoing inquiries.
name: Respond to audit inquiries

description: Keeping a repository of documents that contain information about the network of partners, prospects and customers. Keep records up-to-date with routine reviews and modify as needed.
name: Maintain documentation of contacts

description: Ensuring regular interaction between business entity and quasi-government bodies in order to maintain established relationships. Collect and record meeting data for further analysis and use.
name: Plan and manage meetings

description: Managing relations with organizations established and financed by businesses that operate in a specific industry. Participate in public relations actions such as lobbying and publishing, advertising, education, and political donations.
name: Manage relations with trade or industry groups

description: Determining the requirements to enter in to an agreement with trade or industry agencies. Discover what activities or processes can be conducted to provide the best mutual outcome.
name: Evaluate the requirements for strategic relationships

description: Analyzing current relationships with trade and industry groups. Ensure that the partnership in successful and make modifications where needed.
name: Monitor the success of the partnerships

description: Providing additional information or inclusion for third party trade or industry entities; or changing existing parameters to modify the current relationship. Communicate and execute changes.
name: Extend or change the relationships

description: Managing lobbying activities to affect government policies.
name: Manage lobby activities

description: Maintaining relations with representatives of the stockholders. Establish corporate management-related policies and to make decisions on major company issues. Implement practices designed to engender communication, trust, and cooperation.
name: Manage relations with board of directors

description: Reporting financial results to management, and releasing results to the public. Report financial statements, including the income statement, balance sheet, and statement of cash flows.
name: Report financial results

description: Reporting audit findings to management. Practice an internal audit with criteria for confirming a problem, a description of the situation, and the root cause of the problem. Make recommendations that resolve the issue.
name: Report audit findings

description: Managing legal practices to abide by the law, as well as ethical practices.
name: Manage legal and ethical issues

description: Creating a code of ethics that communicate the organization's philosophy to employees, vendors, customers, clients, and the public.
name: Create ethics policies

description: Administering the system of rules, practices, and processes through which a company is directed and controlled. Balance stakeholder interests including shareholders, management, customers, suppliers, financiers, government, and the community. Outline a strategy for achieving organizational goals, from action plans and internal controls to performance measurement and corporate disclosure.
name: Manage corporate governance policies

description: Creating and applying programs and activities. Encourage the adherence preventive laws, such as environmental law, sex discrimination, computer law, estate planning, corporate compliance, business planning, and property transactions.
name: Develop and perform preventive law programs

description: Ensuring the organization's compliance position. Validate compliance with different statutes, regulatory directions, and legal principles (using Establish compliance framework and policies [17468]). Coordinate with compliance and risk management personnel.
name: Ensure compliance

description: Employing an internal system or process to identify and reduce the risk of breaching the Competition and Consumer Act 2010. Remedy any breach. Create a culture of compliance. Design compliance programs.
name: Plan and initiate compliance program

description: Implementing the established compliance program in order to meet the government laws and regulations. Create a compliance team that scrutinizes the rules set out by government bodies such as the Securities and Exchange Commission.
name: Execute compliance program

description: Managing professionals, sought externally for assistance over legal and ethical concerns. Administer and oversee assistance from subject matter experts and professionals for sourcing expert opinion and counseling over legal and ethical matters.
name: Manage outside counsel

description: Examining the problems and deciding the action requirements for engaging outside counsel. This process element calls upon the organization to internally analyze the issues for which assistance from external counsel is needed. Additionally, the organization needs to break-down the issue, identifying the tasks and exercises where outside counsel can help.
name: Assess problem and determine work requirements

description: Recruiting the assistance of outside counsel for any legal and/or ethical concerns. Engage and/or retain any external counsel sought from subject matter experts and professionals.
name: Engage/Retain outside counsel if necessary

description: Making a financial plan. This strategy sets out, using figures, an organization's expected future results. Enlist the finance function to support work generated by other business functions in order to build and secure their support for the budget.
name: Receive strategy/budget

description: Receiving deliverables from outside counsel, and monitoring the efforts committed by them. Track the progress of, collect, and validate the required work product from the subject matter experts and professionals engaged as external counsel. Verify the amount of effort dedicated by these counsels to the issue at hand, in order to confirm their fees.
name: Receive work product and manage/monitor case and work performed

description: Making payments to legal advisers for their services. Payments include addressing issues or suits by customers, suppliers, competitors, bankers, government agencies, etc.
name: Process payment for legal services

description: Keeping track of the legal activities and performance of the organization.
name: Track legal activity/performance

description: Safeguarding the intellectual property of the organization. This process requires the organization to protect a wide variety of intellectual property created by it. It involves creating and managing non-disclosure agreements (NDAs), following up on current developments in the areas where the organization holds patents, tracking the use of the organization's copyrighted material, creating and upholding licensing terms, and administering policies for safeguarding intellectual property.
name: Protect intellectual property

description: Managing the patents and copyrights already held or sought by the organization. Administer and oversee applying for, securing, and maintaining intellectual property rights in the form of patents and copyrights. Submit applications for such rights. Handle associated legal issues. Draft and communicate proper attributions. Collect royalties. Monitor any misuse of the intellectual property rights.
name: Manage copyrights, patents, and trademarks

description: Managing the upkeep of intellectual property rights by creating and managing a framework of rules, policies, procedures, and restrictions. Outline a clear policy for any possible scenario of their use by any external agent.
name: Maintain intellectual property rights and restrictions

description: Administering and overseeing the terms and policies associated with licensing the organization's intellectual property. Create and manage the policies and terms governing the possible granting of a license to any external agent. Demarcate a clear framework that governs the licensing of any patents or copyrights held by the organization.
name: Administer licensing terms

description: Managing options regarding licensing agreements. Follow favorable terms and conditions.
name: Administer options

description: Resolving disputes or civil lawsuits internally or externally.
name: Resolve disputes and litigations

description: Providing legal advice concerning the substance or procedure of a law in relation to a particular situation.
name: Provide legal advice/counseling

description: Negotiating terms to reach a final draft of a contract that is acceptable to all parties.
name: Negotiate and document agreements/contracts

description: Managing a public relations programs through business and communications skills.
name: Manage public relations program

description: Developing and administering community relations. Establish business connections with the people constituting the environment the organization operates in and draws resources from in order to foster mutual understanding, trust, and support. Create programs that promote the organization's image in a positive and community-oriented way.
name: Manage community relations

description: Developing and managing relations with media. Develop connections with journalists to solicit critical, third-party endorsements for a product, issue, service, or organization.
name: Manage media relations

description: Promoting political security and stability in the regions where the organization conducts business. Encourage political stability in the regions where the organization operates. Support civic programs, citizen engagement, connection platforms, etc.
name: Promote political stability

description: Developing press releases to communicate developments and generate interest in the organization.
name: Create press releases

description: Issuing press releases to carefully selected media in distribution channels such as the web, newspapers, and social media.
name: Issue press releases

description: ITIL 4 practice: Supplier Management. Mapped to Manage External Relationships.
name: Supplier Management

description: Activity within ITIL practice 'Supplier Management'.
name: Evaluate and select suppliers

description: Activity within ITIL practice 'Supplier Management'.
name: Manage supplier contracts

description: Activity within ITIL practice 'Supplier Management'.
name: Monitor supplier performance

description: Performing activities by an organization that are fundamental to the successful operation of the organization, even across functions in a business. Capabilities defined in the PCF include business process management; portfolio, program, and project management; quality management; change management; benchmarking; environmental health and safety management; and knowledge management.
name: Develop and Manage Business Capabilities

description: Establishing and administering governance for management of the processes. Outline and manage the frameworks for management of the processes. Define the business processes. Administer the performance of the processes. Enhance the business processes.
name: Manage business processes

description: Defining and managing the organization's approach to governing business process management. Establish and manage tools to support the governance process. Assign ownership for all business processes. Perform activities to administer the governing process.
name: Establish and maintain process management governance

description: Outlining and managing the methodology for administering business process management (BPM). Define the method for setting standards and priorities for BPM efforts. Identify BPM governance leaders. Define BPM project participants' roles.
name: Define and manage governance approach

description: Instituting, organizing, and maintaining the upkeep of the techniques used for business process management (BPM). Create and maintain templates of BPM tools that can be readily implemented, including process engine, business analytics, content management, and collaboration tools.
name: Establish and maintain process tools and templates

description: Assigning resources (employees) ownership of tasks. These include the responsibility of identifying, analyzing, and improving business processes in order to meet the goals and objectives such as increasing profits and performance, reducing costs, and accelerating schedules.
name: Assign and support process ownership

description: Implementing and executing activities for governing business processes. Execute activities that encourage participation, accountability, transparency, responsiveness, equity and inclusiveness, etc. within the business processes.
name: Perform process governance activities

description: Determining and organizing the structural composition of business processes. Design, establish, and administer the framework. Identify any cross-functional processes that are mandatory for achieving business excellence.
name: Define and manage process frameworks

description: Defining and managing the framework that outlines the required business processes of the organization, key elements, and how they should interact. Institute strategy infrastructure and product, operations, and enterprise management.
name: Establish and maintain process framework

description: Recognizing the different functional areas working on the same project or goal.
name: Identify cross-functional processes

description: Outlining and establishing the business processes of the organization. Scope, analyze, map, and publish processes for the employees who may require it.
name: Define processes

description: Defining the extent and limits of business processes. Define the range and diversity of all the set of activities and tasks that, once completed, will accomplish an organizational goal.
name: Scope processes

description: Assessing and examining the set of activities and tasks that, once completed, will accomplish an organizational goal. Create a business process model that captures how a business process works and how individuals from different groups work together to achieve a business goal.
name: Analyze processes

description: Realizing those practices and procedures that are the most effective to the success of the business and making that information available.
name: Identify published best practices

description: Establishment of a "checkpoint" in a process that prevent the process from continuing unless all requirements are satisfied.
name: Identify and denote process control points

description: Defining what a business entity does, who is responsible, to what standard a business process should be completed, and how the success of a business process can be determined. Identify processes, gather information gathering, interview participants, map processes, and perform analysis.
name: Model and document processes

description: Disclosing the information available on business processes. Ensure the availability of the information regarding the business processes to all process team members, business stakeholders, and process owners. Use BPM software, as well as business process diagrams and documents that help depict the required information.
name: Publish processes

description: Evaluating and handling the performance of business processes. Provide training to process owners. Support the execution of business processes. Measure and report the performance of the business processes.
name: Manage process performance

description: Providing training for the employees and process owners that administer the business processes. Design internal training programs or source third party agencies to provide the skills and training necessary.
name: Provide process training

description: Assisting and executing the business processes. Use business process execution language (BEPL), which is a standard, executable language for specifying actions within the business processes with the use of web services.
name: Support process execution

description: Defining and using performance indicators to consider the financial perspective, customer perspective, internal process perspective, and learning perspective of the organization.
name: Measure and report process performance

description: Determining the need for additional performance indicators that would be necessary to successfully achieve the business goal.
name: Identify additional metrics as required

description: Identifying, selecting, and managing improvements. Based on the type and scope of the improvement, determination of the appropriate improvement methodology (e.g., Lean, Six Sigma, etc.) should guide the effort. This includes continuous improvement, process redesign, process reengineering, and application of the organizations approaches for Manage projects.
name: Improve processes

description: Helping a process owner to identify, analyze, and improve existing business processes within an organization to meet new goals and objectives.
name: Identify and select improvement opportunities

description: Assessing and choosing methodologies to identify, analyze, and improve existing processes within an organization to meet new goals and objective. Assess the various methodologies available such as process mapping, statistical process control, and simulation. Choose the most appropriate and effective methodology.
name: Select process improvement methodology

description: Developing and implementing a systematic approach to help the organization optimize its underlying processes in order to achieve more efficient results. Systematically gather information to clarify issues or problems. Intervene for improvements. Restructure training programs as appropriate to increase effectiveness.
name: Manage improvement projects

description: Persistently implementing activities for improving business processes.
name: Perform continuous improvement activities

description: Managing investments, holdings, products, businesses, and brands, along with the related projects that together constitute a program.
name: Manage portfolio, program, and project

description: Managing the business portfolio of the organization, including investments, holdings, products, businesses, and brands. Establish a portfolio strategy. Define portfolio governance. Monitor and control the portfolio.
name: Manage portfolio

description: Instituting the strategy for managing business portfolio. Create a systematic plan that defines the strategy for managing investments, holdings, products, businesses, and brands.
name: Establish portfolio strategy

description: Outlining the administration of business portfolio of the organization. Create and manage the rules and regulations regarding the business processes in order to identify, select, prioritize, and monitor portfolio components. Include a set of metrics to indicate the health and progress of the portfolio in the most vital area.
name: Define portfolio governance

description: Overseeing and administering the business portfolio of the organization. Monitor all activities related to investments, holdings, products, businesses, and brands by effectively monitoring and supervising these activities.
name: Monitor and control portfolio
